title: Sean Heuvel
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Sean Heuvel
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
published_titles: Associate Dean of Graduate and Professional Programs
biography: Dr. Sean M. Heuvel serves as Associate Dean of Graduate and Professional Programs at Christopher Newport University. In that capacity, he helps oversee CNU's graduate studies programs as well as its continuing education programs. He holds a B.A. in Government, M.Ed. in Higher Education Administration, and Ph.D. in Higher Education Administration from The College of William and Mary. He also completed an M.A. in History from the University of Richmond. Prior to this current position, he served as a faculty member at CNU for sixteen years and was an admissions officer in CNU's Undergraduate Admissions Office before that. An active scholar in the fields of American military history, higher education, and leadership studies, he has written numerous books as well as articles for refereed journals, encyclopedias, and magazines. His interests include higher education history, the college presidency, the American Revolution, the American Civil War, World War II, and military leadership. A participant in foreign study programs at CNU, Heuvel has also lectured at Harris Manchester College, Oxford University, in the United Kingdom. He and his wife and two children live in Williamsburg, Virginia.
education_entries: Ph D in Educational Leadership, The College of William and Mary
MA in History, University of Richmond
M Ed in Educational Leadership, The College of William and Mary
BA in Government, The College of William and Mary
research_interests: Military History and Leadership (American Revolution, U.S. Civil War, World War II), Higher Education Leadership, The College Presidency
teaching_interests: Leadership through the Ages, Senior Leadership Seminar, Theories and Perspectives on Leadership
publications: Journal Article, Academic Journal (2020). New perspectives on heroic/postheroic leadership and on heroic followership. . International Leadership Journal. Volume, 12. Issue, 1. Pages, 8-25.
Book, Scholarly-New (2020). From Across the Sea: North Americans in Nelson's Navy . Helion and Company. Pages, 316.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2018). There is still a place for the study of great leaders. . International Leadership Journal. Volume, 10. Issue, 2.
Book Review (2018). 1777: Tipping Point at Saratoga . History: Reviews of New Books. Volume, 46. Issue, 4. Pages, 96.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2017). "Culture Clash: An Analysis of Dwight D. Eisenhower's Engagement with Academic Culture as President of Columbia University" . Journal of Higher Education Management. Volume, 31. Issue, 2.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2017). "Understanding a New Type of Higher Education Leader: A Call to Arms to Study Nontraditional College Presidents . International Leadership Journal. Volume, 9. Issue, 2. Pages, 80-84.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2016). "Terry Sanford at Duke University: A Leadership Efficacy Model for Non-Traditional College Presidents in Adjusting to Academic Culture" . Journal of Higher Education Management. Volume, 31. Issue, 1. Pages, 98-118.
